title: juncus articulatus subsp. atricapillus
intro: Juncus articulatus subsp. atricapillus, also known as jointed rush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae. It is a type of rush that is native to Europe and is often found growing in wetland areas. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. The jointed rush can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing.
